title: Meta block access: evaluator
analysis:
Task 211 completed all compiler and expression-evaluator work: MetaAccess variant in CompiledExprKind, EvalContext.meta field with with_meta() builder, and the eval_expr arm that looks up meta from context. Tests exist in reify-expr/tests/meta_eval_tests.rs.

What remains (task 212) is wiring meta data from TopologyTemplate.meta into the Engine's evaluation pipeline in reify-eval. Currently, ALL ~25 EvalContext::new() calls in reify-eval/src/lib.rs create contexts WITHOUT meta, meaning any expression containing MetaAccess will panic at runtime.

The implementation requires:
1. Add `meta_map: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>` field to Engine struct
2. In Engine::eval(), build meta_map from module.templates: for each template, insert template.name -> template.meta
3. Wire .with_meta(&meta_map) to all EvalContext::new() calls across 6 functions:
   - eval() main path: field eval (810,815), param defaults (908), guard eval (948,971,1008)
   - evaluate_let_bindings() (2809)
   - elaborate_child_instance() free fn (3113,3115,3181) -- needs meta_map parameter
   - edit_param() (1382,1457,1482,1500,1642,1708,1732,1818)
   - eval_cached() (2119,2212)
   - resolve_concurrent_edit() (702)
4. Add meta_map field to ConcurrentEditSetup for concurrent edit path
5. Pass meta_map to compile_geometry_op() free fn (2852,2903) for completeness

For sub-structure meta access (sub.meta.key): The compiler produces MetaAccess { entity: child_template_name, key } when compiling sub.meta.key. During child elaboration, the child template's expressions use the original entity name (not the scoped name), so the meta_map naturally contains the correct entry via template.name -> template.meta. No special scoping logic needed.

For collection subs: Each indexed instance evaluates against child_values with original entity names, so meta lookup works identically to singular subs.

TopologyTemplateBuilder in reify-test-support currently hard-codes `meta: HashMap::new()` in build(). Need to add a meta() builder method for integration tests.

prerequisites.description: Add `meta()` builder method to TopologyTemplateBuilder in crates/reify-test-support/src/builders.rs. Method signature: `pub fn meta(mut self, entries: HashMap<String, String>) -> Self`. Store entries in a new `meta: HashMap<String, String>` field on the builder (default empty). Use the field in build() instead of hard-coded empty HashMap.

Add `meta_map: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>` field to Engine struct. Initialize as empty HashMap in Engine::new(). This stores the entity-name-to-meta-entries mapping built during eval() and reused by edit_param() and other incremental paths.

steps.description: Write failing test `eval_meta_access_in_let_binding` in new file crates/reify-eval/tests/meta_eval_integration.rs. Build a TopologyTemplate 'Widget' with meta {"description": "A widget"} and a Let binding whose expr is CompiledExpr::meta_access("Widget", "description"). Eval via Engine and assert the let cell's value == Value::String("A widget"). Test will panic because eval() doesn't wire meta into EvalContext.

In Engine::eval(), after module is available: build meta_map from module.templates (filter non-empty .meta, map name->meta). Store as self.meta_map. Wire .with_meta(&self.meta_map) to all EvalContext::new() calls within eval() body: field eval (lines ~810,815), param default (908), guard expr (948), guard members (971,1008). Also wire in evaluate_let_bindings() (2809) -- pass &self.meta_map as new parameter or use self reference.

Write failing test `eval_meta_access_sub_structure` in meta_eval_integration.rs. Build two templates: 'Part' with meta {"material": "steel"} and param size, and 'Assembly' with sub_component 'part' -> 'Part' and a Let binding using CompiledExpr::meta_access("Part", "material"). Eval via Engine and assert the Assembly let cell's value == Value::String("steel"). Tests that child template meta is accessible from parent context.

Pass &self.meta_map to elaborate_child_instance() as a new parameter. Wire .with_meta(meta_map) to the three EvalContext::new() calls inside elaborate_child_instance (child arg eval ~3113, child default ~3115, child let ~3181). Update all call sites of elaborate_child_instance in eval() to pass &self.meta_map.

Write failing test `eval_meta_access_survives_edit_param` in meta_eval_integration.rs. Build a template 'Box' with meta {"tag": "v1"}, a Param 'size' with default 10.0, and a Let 'label' whose expr is meta_access("Box", "tag"). Call engine.eval(), then engine.edit_param(size_cell, new_value). Assert that after edit_param, the 'label' cell still has Value::String("v1").

Wire .with_meta(&self.meta_map) to all EvalContext::new() calls inside edit_param() method: main re-eval (~1382), wave2 after resolution (~1457), guard re-elab members (~1482,1500), resolution wave2 (~1642), second guard re-elab (~1708,1732), collection sub cell eval (~1818).

Write failing test `eval_meta_access_in_guarded_group` in meta_eval_integration.rs. Build template 'S' with meta {"mode": "active"}, a Bool param 'active' (default true), and a guarded group whose member Let 'mode_label' uses meta_access("S", "mode"). Eval via Engine and assert mode_label == Value::String("active") when guard is true.

Verify that step-2's wiring of guard eval EvalContext calls (lines ~948, 971, 1008) already passes this test. If not, ensure the guarded group member expression evaluation at lines 971 and 1008 in eval() uses .with_meta(&self.meta_map). Guarded group evaluation in eval() evaluates member default_exprs directly -- confirm they get meta context.

Write test `eval_meta_access_cached_eval` in meta_eval_integration.rs. Call engine.eval_cached() with a module containing meta access. Assert the let binding resolves to the correct Value::String. This tests the eval_cached() incremental path.

Wire .with_meta(&self.meta_map) to EvalContext::new() calls inside eval_cached() (~2119, 2212). Also wire resolve_concurrent_edit() (~702). Add meta_map field to ConcurrentEditSetup struct, populate it in prepare_concurrent_edit() from self.meta_map. Wire compile_geometry_op() calls (~2852, 2903 and others) by passing meta_map parameter for completeness.

design_decisions.decision: Store meta_map on Engine struct rather than rebuilding per-call
design_decisions.rationale: The meta map is built once from TopologyTemplate.meta during eval() and reused across edit_param(), eval_cached(), resolve_concurrent_edit(), and other incremental paths. Storing on Engine follows the same pattern as `functions: Vec<CompiledFunction>` and `compiled_purposes: Vec<CompiledPurpose>` which are also captured during eval() for later use.

design_decisions.decision: Meta map keyed by template name (not scoped entity names)
design_decisions.rationale: MetaAccess { entity, key } uses the template name as entity (set at compile time). During sub-component elaboration, child expressions are evaluated against child_values with original entity names -- the scoped entity prefix is only used for storing results in the parent's value map. So the meta map naturally needs template names as keys, not scoped names like 'Parent.sub[0]'.

design_decisions.decision: Pass meta_map as parameter to free functions rather than global state
design_decisions.rationale: elaborate_child_instance() and compile_geometry_op() are free functions (not Engine methods). Adding a &HashMap parameter is consistent with how `functions: &[CompiledFunction]` is already passed. This avoids the need for a larger refactor to make these methods on Engine.

design_decisions.decision: Wire ALL EvalContext calls including geometry compilation
design_decisions.rationale: While geometry arguments are typically numeric and unlikely to use MetaAccess, wiring meta to all EvalContext calls ensures correctness if a future expression context uses meta values (e.g., a conditional geometry parameter based on meta). The cost is negligible -- just an additional .with_meta() chain.

design_decisions.decision: Add meta_map to ConcurrentEditSetup for concurrent evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: ConcurrentEditSetup already carries functions, values, and other state needed for off-thread evaluation. Adding meta_map ensures concurrent eval paths can also resolve MetaAccess expressions without accessing the Engine directly.

reuse.how: Use the builder pattern with .meta() to construct test templates with meta blocks. Follow the pattern from guard_eval.rs and collection_sub_eval.rs for building CompiledModules in integration tests.
Chain .with_meta(&meta_map) after EvalContext::new() at every call site. The builder is already implemented and tested in reify-expr.

reuse.where: crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs Engine struct and eval() method
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: add field to Engine, initialize empty in new(), populate in eval() from module data, use in edit_param() and other incremental paths.

reuse.what: Guard eval test pattern from guard_eval.rs
reuse.how: Follow the test structure: build template with TopologyTemplateBuilder, wrap in CompiledModuleBuilder, create MockConstraintChecker, run Engine::eval(), assert values by ValueCellId lookup.
Use CompiledExpr::meta_access(entity, key) to build MetaAccess expressions in test let bindings. Result type is always Type::String.
Follow the pattern for building multi-template modules with sub-components: parent template with sub_component(), child template in same module, verify cross-template evaluation.
